title: Cisco OSPF Multi-Area
description: Configure a multi-area OSPF design on an ABR with stub and totally stubby areas, inter-area summarization, virtual links, and external route summarization.

tasks.prompt: Enter Global Configuration mode on ABR-R2 to configure multi-area OSPF.
tasks.explanation: Area border routers (ABRs) connect the backbone area 0 to one or more non-backbone areas.

tasks.prompt: Enable OSPF routing process 1 on the ABR.
tasks.explanation: The ABR runs a single OSPF process and maintains separate link-state databases for each connected area.

tasks.prompt: Set the OSPF router ID to 2.2.2.2 on the ABR.
tasks.explanation: A stable router ID is required for virtual-link negotiation and consistent LSA origination across areas.

tasks.prompt: Advertise all 10.0.x.x backbone links into OSPF area 0.
tasks.explanation: Area 0 is the OSPF backbone. All other areas must connect to area 0 directly or through a virtual link.

tasks.prompt: Advertise all 10.1.x.x interfaces into OSPF area 1.
tasks.explanation: Non-backbone areas reduce LSA flooding scope. The ABR translates Type 1 and Type 2 LSAs into Type 3 summary LSAs.

tasks.prompt: Advertise all 10.2.x.x interfaces into OSPF area 2.
tasks.explanation: Each area has its own SPF calculation. ABRs sit at the boundary and exchange inter-area routes.

tasks.prompt: Configure area 1 as a stub area to block external Type 5 LSAs.
tasks.explanation: Stub areas do not accept external routes. The ABR injects a default inter-area route instead.

tasks.prompt: Configure area 2 as a totally stubby area that blocks inter-area and external routes.
tasks.explanation: Totally stubby areas (stub no-summary) block Type 3, Type 4, and Type 5 LSAs. Only a default route is advertised into the area.

tasks.prompt: Summarize area 1 routes at the ABR using range 10.1.0.0/16.
tasks.explanation: Inter-area route summarization at the ABR replaces multiple Type 3 LSAs with a single summary prefix.

tasks.prompt: Create a virtual link through area 0 to reach the remote ABR with router ID 1.1.1.1.
tasks.explanation: A virtual link extends area 0 through a transit area to connect a partitioned backbone to the main area 0.

tasks.prompt: Summarize external routes at the ASBR using summary-address 10.0.0.0 255.0.0.0.
tasks.explanation: External route summarization aggregates redistributed routes into fewer Type 5 LSAs at the ASBR.

tasks.prompt: Set the default route cost injected into stub area 1 to 25.
tasks.explanation: The default-cost command controls the metric of the default inter-area route the ABR advertises into a stub area.

tasks.prompt: Exit OSPF router configuration mode back to privileged EXEC mode.
tasks.explanation: Use 'end' to return directly to privileged EXEC mode for verification commands.

tasks.prompt: Display all area border routers known to this router.
tasks.explanation: This command lists ABRs and ASBRs in the OSPF domain, confirming the router's role at area boundaries.

tasks.prompt: Verify the status of OSPF virtual links.
tasks.explanation: Virtual-link output shows the transit area, remote router ID, and adjacency state across the logical backbone extension.
